author: Victualed
text: Scope reduction during implementation: ship only §11.1 (pre-XDG → nested XDG). The ball description named two additional paths that turn out to be impossible: (a) §11.2 'bl-ed32 two-file → three-file split' — the SPEC has evolved; current §11.2 is hashed-XDG → nested, and no binary ever wrote workspace.json under the bl-ed32 schema (grep workspace.json src/ returns nothing). (b) hashed-XDG layout — reverted in bl-77cb before any release shipped (grep <origin-key>|<path-hash> returns SPEC self-refs only). Combined-history requires hashed-XDG to have shipped, so it cannot exist in practice. The omission is documented in src/migrate.rs module docs so a future ball can pick up defense-in-depth if a never-shipped layout is ever observed.

author: Victualed
text:
Phase 2: bl migrate — pre-XDG → nested XDG conversion

Adds the explicit, idempotent, refuses-if-dirty bl migrate subcommand per SPEC-clone-layout §11.1. Detects pre-XDG layout (.balls/config.json on main + .balls/state-repo runtime + .balls-worktrees colocated), translates .balls/config.json to repo.json on the orphan branch (with delivery→integrate and review.pre_check→review.gate_command field renames + value translations local-squash→direct, deferred→forge-pr per §6.6), optionally writes tracker.json when the legacy carried a state_url, copies .balls/local/{claims,lock,plugins} to the XDG per-clone tree, git-worktree-moves task worktrees to ~/.local/state/balls/worktrees/<nested>/, then commits the on-main cleanup (the last balls-attributed commit on main for the clone's lifetime).

Scope reduction: ships only §11.1 (real-world clones). §11.2 hashed-XDG was reverted in bl-77cb before release, never shipped. bl-ed32 workspace.json schema was SPEC-only, no binary ever wrote it. Combined-history requires hashed-XDG to have shipped, so cannot exist. Documented in src/migrate.rs module docs; a future ball can pick up defense-in-depth if needed.

Cross-doc rename ridealong (SPEC §6.5.1): SPEC-forge-gated-delivery.md §5/§7/§8/§9/§10 use integrate.mode/direct/forge-pr and review.gate_command throughout; the repo-level target_branch documentation reflects its §6.7 removal. SPEC-tracker-state §7 config-ownership table updated to new field names.

Conformance tests: §14.21 (one-shot pre-XDG migration), §14.23 (post-migration fallback never reappears), §14.16/§14.17 post-migration branches (repo.json carries new field names). Plus refuses-if-dirty (main + per-worktree), federated state_url → tracker.json, partial-migration retry, target_branch warning, stray non-dir under .balls-worktrees/.
